headline: Minister's political secretary approved RM290 million in contracts
context: A political secretary to the Works Minister was found to have approved RM290 million in road maintenance contracts despite having no legal procurement authority.

cards.big: Political secretary signed off on RM290 million in contracts — with no authority to do so
cards.sub: Unelected aide wielded procurement power meant only for senior civil servants

cards.big: Treasury Instruction 167 reserves procurement approval for designated officers only
cards.sub: Political secretaries are not authorised signatories under financial procedures

cards.big: 14 of 23 approved contracts went to companies linked to the secretary's associates
cards.sub: Direct connections between approver and beneficiaries suggest systematic patronage

cards.big: Political secretaries have no formal job description defined in any legislation
cards.sub: Shadow power operates without accountability framework or code of conduct

cards.big: When political aides control procurement, elected officials get plausible deniability
Should political appointees be legally barred from any role in government procurement?
